LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CARPET CONTRACTORS, INC., NICHOLAS BOTAITIS & DEMETRI BOTAITIS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND, 3RD, 5TH, 6TH, 7TH, 8TH, 9TH AND 12TH CAUSES OF ACTION, OVERRULED AS TO THE 1ST, 4TH, 10TH AND 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE 6TH, 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED COMPLIANCE WITH LAB. CODE 2699.3(A). (SEE LAB. CODE 2699.5) FOR THE 2ND, 3RD, 5TH, 6TH, 7TH, 8TH, 9TH AND 12TH CAUSES OF ACTION, MR. SIERRA'S LABOR CLAIMS ARE BARRED BY RES JUDICATA. SEE NOBLE V. DRAPER (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1, 11) ("RES JUDICATA BARS CLAIMS THAT COULD HAVE BEEN RAISED IN THE FIRST PROCEEDING REGARDLESS OF WHETHER OR NOT THEY WERE RAISED [CITATION], COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL BARS ONLY ISSUES THAT WERE ACTUALLY AND NECESSARILY DECIDED IN THE EARLIER LITIGATION. [CTATION.]" MR. SIERRA SOUGHT RELIEF FOR SOME OF HIS LABOR CLAIMS BEFORE THE LABOR COMMISSION FOR HIS EMPLOYMENT WITH DEFENDANTS FROM 1994 TO 2012. MR. SIERRA COULD HAVE RAISED ALL OF HIS LABOR CODE VIOLATION CLAIMS FROM HIS EMPLOYMENT FROM 1990 TO 2012 WITH THE LABOR COMMISSION. (SEE COMPLAINT, 7.) THE 1ST, 4TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION COULD NOT HAVE BEEN RAISED BEFORE THE LABOR COMMISSION, AND RES JUDICATA DOES NOT APPLY. (NOBLE, SUPRA, 160 CAL.APP.4TH AT 12.) THE PLEADINGS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER'S DECISION THAT IS ATTACHED TO THE OPPOSITION. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO HEAR THE DEMURRER EVEN THOUGH IT WAS NOT TIMELY FILED. NO DEFAULT WAS TAKEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR7489.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Guillermo Sierra and Raul Paz's motion for summary adjudication is denied without prejudice to being refiled after a motion for class certification has been adjudicated. Defendants Carpet Contractors, Inc., Kostas Botaitis, Nicholas D. Botaitis, and Demetri N. Botaitis's objection to resolution of the merits prior to resolution of class certification per the Home Savings rule is meritorious. Plaintiffs have not shown a compelling justification for having this motion heard before a ruling on class certification. The case law cited by plaintiffs in their reply memorandum does not state or suggest that the Home Savings rule is inapplicable to a case such as this one where the plaintiffs allege both traditional class action claims and PAGA claims. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)

Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend as to the 2nd, 3rd, 5th, 6th, 7th, 8th, 9th and 12th causes of action, overruled as to the 1st, 4th, 10th and 11th causes of action. For the 6th, 7th and 8th causes of action, Plaintiff has not pled compliance with Lab. Code 2699.3(a). (See Lab. Code 2699.5) For the 2nd, 3rd, 5th, 6th, 7th, 8th, 9th and 12th causes of action, Mr. Sierra's labor claims are barred by res judicata. See Noble v. Draper (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1, 11) ("res judicata bars claims that could have been raised in the first proceeding regardless of whether or not they were raised [citation], collateral estoppel bars only issues that were actually and necessarily decided in the earlier litigation. [ctation.]" Mr. Sierra sought relief for some of his labor claims before the Labor Commission for his employment with Defendants from 1994 to 2012. Mr. Sierra could have raised all of his labor code violation claims from his employment from 1990 to 2012 with the Labor Commission. (See Complaint, 7.) The 1st, 4th and 10th causes of action could not have been raised before the Labor Commission, and res judicata does not apply. (Noble, supra, 160 Cal.App.4th at 12.) The pleadings are not uncertain. The Court takes judicial notice of the Labor Commissioner's Decision that is attached to the opposition. The Court exercises its discretion to hear the demurrer even though it was not timely filed. No default was taken. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.
